author: cms-tsg-storm
body:
backport of https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/pull/50962

#### PR description:

Development of HLT menus for `CMSSW_16_0_X`:
   * This is the PR for the `16_1_X branch` (this is the release cycle currently used for Run-3 HLT-menu development).

These updates correspond to the updates corresponding to the first version "**V1.0" of the HIon menu** for the 2026 PbPb data-taking.
This update includes the integration of the following JIRA tickets:
- [CMSHLT-3646](https://its.cern.ch/jira/browse/CMSHLT-3646) [HIN,TRK] Move `hltPixelTracksPPOnAASoA` and `hltPixelVerticesPPOnAASoA` to run on alpaka backend
- [CMSHLT-3822](https://its.cern.ch/jira/browse/CMSHLT-3822) [HIN] UPC paths for `L1_DoubleEG3_DeltaPhi2p(0,5)_NotMinimumBiasHF2_AND_BptxAND` in HIon table
- [CMSHLT-3826](https://its.cern.ch/jira/browse/CMSHLT-3826) [HIN] Extra HLT paths for UPC monopole triggers
- [CMSHLT-3827](https://its.cern.ch/jira/browse/CMSHLT-3827) [HIN] HLT passthroughs for beam-background monitoring seeds in HIon
- [CMSHLT-3830](https://its.cern.ch/jira/browse/CMSHLT-3830) [HIN] Strategy for introducing variable pixel-cluster threshold (X) for low-pT tracking triggers
- [CMSHLT-3841](https://its.cern.ch/jira/browse/CMSHLT-3841) [NGT,HIN] Implement the HLT test stand path in HIon menu

In addition this PR introduces the following commits:
- 3964d3281104aa44ca92063a980d370ecde58ce2 : update `hltPathOwners.json` for HIon menu 2026 V1.0
- 0f71f674649e657674007a133c709a4e170392fd: smart path versioning for HIon 2026 V1.0 menu 
- 0d5696a4bc3048f74cb99b0fecb70c8a969199aa: update TSG scripts for 2026 HIon menu

#### PR validation:

TSG integration tests

#### If this PR is a backport please specify the original PR and why you need to backport that PR. If this PR will be backported please specify to which release cycle the backport is meant for:

backport of https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/pull/50962 to the current TSG production cycle (`CMSSW_16_1_X`)
title: HLT menu development for `16_1_X` (1/N)
